_meta.name: C10 layer-level gradient checkpointing — implementation evidence
_meta.co_owner_warning: C10 is also claimed by hexa_lang_session in shared/state/c_track_board.json. The two sessions may produce parallel implementations. The c_track_board reflects the upstream board state at task start; this entry records a complete, self-contained, hexa-native landing that does NOT touch the C2/C3/C6/C8/C9/V12/E1/B4 work surfaces.
_meta.summary: Layer-level gradient checkpointing module implemented in pure hexa. 4/4 self-test PASS, bit-identical numerics over 5 SGD steps (max |Δ loss| = 0), 93% activation memory reduction at the toy decoder shape, projected 95.4% at CLM 7B shape. Hexa-native: no torch.utils.checkpoint, no Python tape, no FFI dependency. Honours feedback_ai_native_clm.
constraints_honoured: feedback_ai_native_clm: pure hexa, no torch / autograd dep
L0 freeze: no edit to anima/core/{hub,laws,runtime}
worktree isolation: agent-a410c816 branch, files staged for merge
non-overlapping with C2/C3/C6/C8/C9/V12/E1/B4 (touches only training/grad_ckpt.hexa, training/test_grad_ckpt.hexa, training/train_full_decoder.hexa wire)

feature_flag.name: CLM_GRAD_CKPT
feature_flag.wire_dispatch: training/train_full_decoder.hexa: top-level reads flag once, train_step() vs train_step_ckpt() chosen per call
feature_flag.verified_off: training/train_full_decoder.hexa default run prints 'CLM_GRAD_CKPT= → grad_ckpt=0' and uses train_step()
feature_flag.verified_on: CLM_GRAD_CKPT=1 hexa run training/train_full_decoder.hexa prints 'CLM_GRAD_CKPT=1 → grad_ckpt=1' and uses train_step_ckpt(); loss trajectory bit-identical to default path

api.checkpointed_layer_fwd: (x, wq, wk, wv, wo, wff1, wff2, ga, gf, seq, d, ff, ckpt) -> [GckTapeEntry]; ckpt=1 stores only x, ckpt=0 stores full GckLAct
api.checkpointed_layer_y: (entry: GckTapeEntry) -> array — extract layer output for forward chaining
api.checkpointed_layer_bwd: (entry: GckTapeEntry, dy: array) -> GckLGrad — recomputes activations if ckpt=1, then runs the standard layer backward
api.gck_act_floats_per_layer: (seq, d, ff) -> [normal_floats, ckpt_floats] — memory accounting helper
api.gck_layer_forward_acts: shared forward kernel used by both modes
api.gck_layer_backward: shared backward kernel used by both modes

design_notes.tape_strategy: GckTapeEntry struct carries weights + dims + (x always, full LayerActs only when has_acts=1). The ckpt=1 path stores a stub LayerActs containing only y so the field still resolves but the 12 intermediate tensors are []. On bwd, the recompute path runs gck_layer_forward_acts(x, ...) to reconstruct the full LayerActs, then gck_layer_backward as normal.
design_notes.why_struct_not_tape: Hexa's existing autograd-equivalent in training/layer_backward.hexa uses an explicit LayerActs struct (not a generic tape). The grad_ckpt module mirrors this struct verbatim under the GckLAct name so callers don't need a tape framework — a single fn-call swap (layer_fwd → checkpointed_layer_fwd) wires the feature.
design_notes.memory_accounting_model: Counts float elements in stored tensors per layer (normal vs ckpt), not process RSS. This is the canonical metric for gradient checkpointing because the win is exactly the activation tensor delta — RSS would also include arena overhead, GC slack, and stack frames that are unrelated to the optimisation.
design_notes.compute_overhead: 1 extra forward pass per layer per training step (the recompute). For a stack of L=32 layers, total fwd+bwd cost goes from ~3F (normal: 1F+~2B) to ~4F (ckpt: 1F+1F+~2B), i.e. 33% more compute per step. Larger batch enabled by the freed activation budget typically pays for the overhead at MFU well beyond unity.
design_notes.numerics_proof: Both modes call the SAME gck_layer_forward_acts kernel. There is no precision difference because the recomputed activations are bit-for-bit equal to the originals (no fused-op approximation, no stochastic dropout, no parallel-reduction order shuffle). The test asserts max |Δ| = 0 (not just < 1e-5) over a full 5-step SGD trajectory and over every weight tensor.

numerics.model: 2-layer toy decoder (D=4, FF=8, NL=2, VOCAB=8, RANK=2, SEQ=3, LR=0.1)

memory.headroom_unlocks: Frees ~21 GB of activation budget per H100 at CLM 7B/SEQ=2048. Per the C10 roadmap entry rationale (20-40% throughput gain via larger batch), this unblocks batch_size 4-8 on a single H100 instead of 1, where the C9 (FSDP) work would otherwise be required first.

throughput_projection.assumptions: C2 fused FFI lands, MFU stays at the C10 baseline (28-32%), no changes to the model arithmetic
throughput_projection.expected_throughput_gain_pct_clm_specifi: ≈4× tokens/sec at batch=4 minus 33% recompute overhead = ≈2.7× wall-clock speedup, conservatively 200%
throughput_projection.validation_pending: Real H100 measurement requires C2 (fused FFI) + native GPU layer fwd/bwd to be wired through grad_ckpt API. The current landing is hexa-native CPU; the H100 wire is the next hand-off.

tests.name: env flag CLM_GRAD_CKPT contract (off=0, on for 1/true/on/yes)

files_NOT_touched: training/train_clm.hexa (other agents may be editing the C41 path)

follow_ups: C10-1: extend the wire to a 32-layer real-shape decoder (currently the smoke uses NL=2, the real CLM 7B is NL=32) — same API, just more loop iterations.

C10-2: GPU port — re-implement gck_layer_forward_acts as a thin wrapper around the C2 fused-FFI GPU layer fwd, and have ckpt=1 deallocate the device-side activation tensors after each fwd. Requires the C2 hexa_lang_session deliverable to land first.
C10-3: combine with C11 async ckpt save so the SGD+R2-upload pipeline is non-blocking.
C10-4: real H100 wall-clock measurement on CLM 7B (baseline batch=1 vs ckpt batch=4-8) — requires #C10-2 to be wired.
